questions.question: What is the optimal window for asking for advocacy after a customer success milestone?
questions.explanation: The 48-72 hour window captures customers when they're emotionally high, results are fresh, and advocacy feels natural. Response rates are 60-70% in this window vs 15-25% after.

questions.question: Which advocacy ask is most appropriate for a Day 30 milestone (first value achieved)?
questions.explanation: Written testimonials are low-friction (2-3 minutes) and appropriate for early wins. Case studies and video testimonials require deeper results and stronger relationships.

questions.question: What is the maximum frequency for advocacy asks to avoid overwhelming customers?
questions.explanation: Maximum one ask per month prevents advocacy fatigue. Space asks across the customer journey (Day 30, 60, 90, renewal) to build advocacy gradually.

questions.question: A customer declines your testimonial request at Day 30. What should you do?

options.text: Flag in CRM and re-ask after next milestone (60-90 days)
Send automated follow-up emails until they respond

questions.explanation: Respect the decline, flag in CRM, and re-ask after the next major milestone when they may have bigger results and more confidence to advocate.

questions.question: Why do personal founder emails for advocacy get 3x the response rate of automated templates?

options.text: Personal emails reference specific milestones and celebrate individual success
Customers feel obligated to respond to founders

questions.explanation: Personal emails that reference specific achievements and celebrate individual success feel genuine, not corporate. Customers respond to being seen and valued.
